name: Azuga Fleet Telematics API
what_it_does: Azuga (Bridgestone) fleet telematics REST API for GPS tracking, driver safety, ELD compliance, and fleet intelligence for commercial and insurance fleets. Enables AI agents to manage real-time vehicle GPS tracking and fleet visibility for fleet operations automation, handle driver safety scoring and gamification for driver behavior improvement programs, access ELD HOS compliance data for FMCSA regulatory compliance, retrieve vehicle diagnostic and OBD-II fault data for predictive maintenance automation, manage geofence zone configuration and alert management for territory and delivery automation, handle insurance telematics data and UBI (usage-based insurance) scoring for insurance fleet programs, access driver reward and incentive tracking for safety culture programs, retrieve fuel consumption and idling analytics for fleet efficiency optimization, manage fleet reporting and trend analytics for fleet performance intelligence, and integrate Azuga with insurance platforms, TMS, and HR systems.
use_cases: Tracking vehicles from fleet visibility agents
Scoring driver safety from fleet safety agents
Monitoring ELD compliance from DOT compliance agents
Enabling UBI scoring from insurance telematics agents
not_for: Heavy commercial trucking requiring specialized ELD hardware
Consumer vehicle tracking without commercial fleet focus
Air or rail transportation without road vehicle telematics
best_when: A commercial fleet operator, insurance company, or SMB field service organization wanting AI agents to automate vehicle tracking, driver safety scoring, ELD compliance monitoring, and usage-based insurance data collection.
avoid_when: FMCSA ELD HOS COMPLIANCE ACCURACY: Automated ELD HOS reporting from Azuga must accurately capture driving time and duty status changes; ELD data used for DOT compliance must reflect actual driver activity; automated HOS errors create FMCSA violation and out-of-service risk. Driver privacy and insurance adverse action — automated UBI scoring used in insurance underwriting decisions must comply with state insurance regulations on telematics-based rating; adverse action using telematics data without required disclosure creates state insurance regulatory exposure. Location data retention minimization — Azuga granular GPS location data retained beyond operational needs creates state location privacy liability; implement automated retention schedule for raw location data. Employee monitoring notice — automated driver behavior monitoring must comply with state-specific employee monitoring notice requirements for fleet operators; monitoring without disclosure creates labor law exposure.

auth.notes: Azuga uses OAuth 2.0 and API key authentication. REST API with JSON. San Jose, California HQ. Founded 2012. Subsidiary of Bridgestone (acquired 2021). Bridgestone Americas/Firestone parent. Commercial fleet and insurance telematics focus. OBD-II plug-in device and OEM telematics. FMCSA-registered ELD. Insurance UBI program partnerships. Competes with Samsara, Lytx, and Fleet Complete for fleet telematics.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — per-vehicle monthly subscription with hardware
pricing.paid_tiers: Per-vehicle monthly subscription; OBD-II device + software; insurance telematics program pricing separate
pricing.notes: San Jose CA. Bridgestone subsidiary (acquired 2021). Founded 2012. OBD-II telematics device. Insurance UBI partnerships. FMCSA ELD registered. Per-vehicle subscription.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per API key; real-time location streaming limits per vehicle count

known_agent_gotchas: FMCSA ELD HOS DUTY STATUS ACCURACY: Automated ELD data processing must correctly identify automatic vs manual duty status changes; FMCSA requires driver-confirmed edit annotations for automated status corrections; automated HOS without driver annotation workflow creates ELD violation

Bridgestone acquisition API stability — Azuga was acquired by Bridgestone in 2021; verify current API investment and product roadmap under Bridgestone ownership; platform integration with Bridgestone fleet services may change API architecture

Insurance UBI adverse rating disclosure — automated insurance telematics scoring used in UBI rating decisions must comply with state insurance department telematics rating regulations; adverse rate action without consumer disclosure and state filing approval creates regulatory violation

OBD-II device data quality — Azuga OBD-II plug-in device data quality depends on vehicle OBD port compatibility and device fit; automated data collection from incompatible OBD configurations produces unreliable telematics data; validate device compatibility before automated data reliance

Driver safety gamification opt-out — automated driver safety scoring and leaderboard programs must honor driver opt-out requests per state law and collective bargaining agreements; automated enrollment without consent creates NLRA and state labor law exposure

Geofence alert deduplication — Azuga geofence entry/exit webhook events may fire multiple times for vehicles lingering near geofence boundaries; implement geofence event deduplication with vehicle ID + zone + minimum dwell time to prevent duplicate dispatch triggers
agent_readiness.security_notes: Fleet telematics. SOC2, FMCSA ELD. OAuth2/API key. US. Driver location, safety, and telematics data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Fleet telematics. SOC2, FMCSA ELD. OAuth2/API key. US. Location and driver behavior data.
